howItWorks.intro: This wood shrinkage calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Shrinkage % = Species Tangential % × (MC Change ÷ Fiber Saturation Point)
Fiber Saturation Point ≈ 19% for most hardwoods
Seasonal Range ≈ Width × Tangential% × (4% MC swing ÷ 19)
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much does wood shrink when it dries?
faq.answer: Wood shrinks about 1% for every 4% change in moisture content (varies by species). A 6-inch red oak board might shrink 0.06" going from 12% to 8% MC — enough to open joints.

faq.question: Does wood move more in some directions?
faq.answer: Yes. Tangential shrinkage (across growth rings) is about twice radial shrinkage. Quartersawn boards move roughly half as much as flatsawn boards across their width.

faq.question: What moisture content should furniture wood be?
faq.answer: For indoor furniture in most US climates: 6-9% MC. Heated homes in winter can drop to 4-6%. Outdoor projects should be at 12-16% MC depending on climate.

faq.question: Is the Wood Shrinkage Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Wood Shrinkage Calculator?
sections.body: The Wood Shrinkage Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your board width, starting moisture content, final moisture content, you get instant results including expected shrinkage, final width, percentage change. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting expected shrinkage right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your board width and need to find the right expected shrinkage. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Board Width (inches)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your board width — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: starting moisture content, final moisture content, wood species. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your expected shrinkage, final width, percentage change. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your board width before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both expected shrinkage and final width — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your wood species, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
